payload.prompt_preview:
You are agent 'researcher' in a fixed benchmark pipeline.
Role: Gather the best sources and extract evidence.
Operate only inside the workspace and do not mention hidden evaluation materials.
Leave a concise output that the next agent can use.

payload.output_preview:
Short answer (snapshot as of 15 March 2026)

1) Most recent enforcement milestone
- The most recent set of AI Act rules that became applicable was the governance and the obligations for providers of general‑purpose AI (GPAI) models, which came into effect on 2 August 2025. The AI Act’s earlier “first rules” (the definition of an AI system, AI literacy and a small set of prohibited AI practices) already applied from 2 February 2025. ([digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/faqs/navigating-ai-act))

Note: a Commission implementing‑act deadline (to publish a template for post‑market monitoring plans for high‑risk systems) fell on 2 February 2026. That deadline has passed; I could not find a clear Commission publication of the specific implementing act/template in the sources searched (see “uncertainties” below). ([ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu](https://ai-act-service-desk.ec.europa.eu/en/ai-act/article-72?utm_source=openai))

2) Which obligations apply ...<truncated>

payload.output_preview:
Snapshot (as of 2026-03-15) — concise answers, source notes, and an artifact saved in the workspace.

1) Most recent enforcement milestone
- What happened: The EU’s earliest “first rules” under the AI Act became applicable on 2 Feb 2025 (definitions, AI literacy, certain prohibited AI practices). Additional governance and obligations specific to providers of general‑purpose AI (GPAI) models became applicable on 2 Aug 2025. (These are the most recent application milestones that are already in force.)
- Key primary sources: European Commission FAQ (“Navigating the AI Act”) and Commission press release on GPAI guidance (18 Jul 2025), plus the AI Act legal text (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) for the staggered timetable.
- Short source notes:
  - European Commission — “Navigating the AI Act” (FAQ). Accessed 2026-03-15. (lists 2 Feb 2025, 2 Aug 2025, 2 Aug 2026 dates)
  - Commission press — “Commission publishes guidelines for providers of general‑purpose AI models.” 2025-07-18.
  - Regulation ...<truncated>

Verdict
- Acceptable with minor follow-up: the previous answer covers the three questions, cites multiple independent sources (including primary EU sources), includes dated source notes, and explicitly states uncertainty/timing ambiguity.

Strengths
- Answers all three tasks: most recent enforcement milestone, next obligations, and key uncertainties.
- Uses multiple sources including Commission pages/press releases and the Official Journal (AI Act regulation).
- Explicitly flags timing/implementation uncertainty and practical friction (implementing acts, standards, notified bodies).
- Saved a structured artifact in the workspace: workspaces/research_summary_eu_ai_act_2026-03-15.json
